_publications._publicationTitle: A platform for rapid patient-derived cutaneous neurofibroma organoid establishment and screening

_contextSnippet: There were variable proportions of cells in the parental cNFs, with a dominant population of S100+ Schwann cells (19%–48% across samples) followed by CD34+ fibroblasts (18%–35%) and a smaller population of c-Kit+ cells (3%–9%)
details: Flow cytometry of parental cNF tumor and organoid showed variable cell-type proportions across NF0001-NF0012 cohort: S100+ Schwann cells dominant (19-48%), CD34+ fibroblasts (18-35%), c-Kit+ mast cells minor population (3-9%); organoids closely recapitulate cellular composition of parental tumors
